option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
FROM openjdk:17-jdk-slim
WORKDIR /app
COPY target/*.jar app.jar
EXPOSE 8761
ENTRYPOINT ["java", "-jar", "app.jar"]

option.name: updatedContent
updatedContent
option.value:
# SSL Certificate Issue - Solutions
## Problem You're encountering: `javax.net.ssl.SSLHandshakeException: PKIX path building failed`
This occurs when Java cannot validate SSL certificates for HTTPS/TLS connections (Gmail SMTP or Config Server GitHub).
## Solutions Implemented
### 1. Mail Configuration Class (MailConfig.java) Created a custom JavaMailSender bean with SSL trust configuration that: - Explicitly trusts the SMTP host - Disables strict server identity checking - Configures TLS protocols properly
### 2. Updated application.yml Ensured all necessary SMTP SSL properties are configured.
## Alternative Solutions (If the above doesn't work)
### Option A: Import Gmail Certificate to Java Truststore
Run these commands in your terminal (as Administrator):
1. Download the certificate: ```cmd echo | openssl s_client -connect smtp.gmail.com:587 -starttls smtp 2>&1 | sed -ne '/-BEGIN CERTIFICATE-/,/-END CERTIFICATE-/p' > gmail.crt ```
2. Import to Java truststore: ```cmd keytool -import -alias gmail-smtp -keystore "%JAVA_HOME%\lib\security\cacerts" -file gmail.crt -storepass changeit -noprompt ```
### Option B: Disable SSL Verification (NOT RECOMMENDED for production)
Add JVM argument when starting the application: ``` -Djavax.net.ssl.trustStore=NONE ```
Or add this to your application startup: ``` -Djavax.net.ssl.trustAll=true ```
### Option C: Use App Password for Gmail
If you're using Gmail with 2FA, you need an App Password: 1. Go to Google Account settings 2. Security → 2-Step Verification → App passwords 3. Generate a new app password 4. Use that password in SPRING_MAIL_PASSWORD environment variable
### Option D: Add SSL debugging
To diagnose the exact issue, add this JVM argument: ``` -Djavax.net.debug=ssl:handshake ```
## Recommended Next Steps
1. Rebuild and restart the notification-service 2. Ensure SPRING_MAIL_USERNAME and SPRING_MAIL_PASSWORD are set correctly 3. If using Gmail with 2FA, use an App Password 4. Check the logs for any remaining SSL errors
## For Config Server Issues
If the SSL error is coming from the config-server connecting to GitHub:
Add this to config-server application properties: ```yaml spring: cloud: config: server: git: skip-ssl-validation: true # Only for development! ```
